sections.paragraph: ἀπολύτρωσις, εως, ἡ [ἀπολυτρόω (ἀπό, λύτρον ‘price of release’) ‘release on payment of ransom’] ‘freedom/liberation from an oppressive circumstance’, release Lk 21:28; Hb 11:35 (under torture). In imagery suggesting deliverance from enslavement through payment of a ransom but with focus on deliverance as such: Ro 3:24; 8:23; Eph 1:7, 14; 4:30; Hb 9:15; of Christ as personification of ἀ. 1 Cor 1:30.
